root_text.an5.81:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ appiyo ca hoti amanāpo ca agaru ca abhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.81:1.3: Rajanīye rajjati, dussanīye dussati, mohanīye muyhati, kuppanīye kuppati, madanīye majjati—
root_text.an5.81:1.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ appiyo ca hoti amanāpo ca agaru ca abhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.81:2.1: Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ piyo ca hoti manāpo ca garu ca bhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.81:2.3: Rajanīye na rajjati, dussanīye na dussati, mohanīye na muyhati, kuppanīye na kuppati, madanīye na majjati—
root_text.an5.81:2.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ piyo ca hoti manāpo ca garu ca bhāvanīyo cā”ti.

translation_text.an5.81:1.1: “Mendicants, a senior mendicant with five qualities is unlikable and disagreeable to their spiritual companions, not respected or admired.
translation_text.an5.81:1.3: They desire the desirable, they hate the hateful, they’re deluded by the delusory, they’re annoyed by the annoying, and they’re intoxicated by the intoxicating.
translation_text.an5.81:1.4: A senior mendicant with these five qualities is unlikable and disagreeable by their spiritual companions, not respected or admired.
translation_text.an5.81:2.1: A senior mendicant with five qualities is dear and beloved to their spiritual companions, respected and admired.
translation_text.an5.81:2.3: They don’t desire the desirable, they don’t hate the hateful, they’re not deluded by the delusory, they’re not annoyed by the annoying, and they’re not intoxicated by the intoxicating.
translation_text.an5.81:2.4: A senior mendicant with these five qualities is dear and beloved to their spiritual companions, respected and admired.”

variant_text.an5.81:1.3: dussanīye → dusanīye (si, sya-all, km, pts1ed) | mohanīye → muhanīye (si) | kuppanīye → kupanīye (si, sya-all, km); kopanīye (pts1ed)
